summary: Initial Level 2 remediation campaign for ls-oneup using module-based workstreams and audit-only ASVS artifacts.

workstreams.goal: Harden edge headers, transport expectations, and deployment-visible request/response policy.
Tighten validation, authorization, and business-rule enforcement.
Close storage-side data protection and submission security gaps.
Align browser-visible behavior with the server-side security posture once the edge is stabilized.
Keep tests and requirement docs aligned with implementation slices.

control_mapping_rules.rationale: These controls mainly land at the worker edge, response headers, and deployment/runtime transport expectations.
These controls primarily change validation, request semantics, upload acceptance rules, and server-side authorization or business logic.
These controls primarily touch storage, submission confidentiality, minimization, and defensive handling close to persistence and crypto.
These controls have a browser-facing implementation surface and should only be claimed when there is actual UI or asset behavior to change.
These controls are mostly evidence, operator guidance, dependency posture, logging, and verification work spanning tests plus requirement docs.

first_wave_slices.goal: Establish edge header policy and transport documentation expectations.
done_when: Edge header behavior is implemented or explicitly documented where deployment-only.
Integration coverage exists for changed route or header behavior.
Planner or operator decisions are recorded for deployment-only controls.
handoff_to_auditor: List changed headers and affected routes.
Identify transport controls ready for re-audit.

first_wave_slices.goal: Tighten request validation, authorization, and business-rule boundaries.
done_when: Validation and authorization rules are explicit at server boundaries.
Affected request semantics are reflected in contract docs and tests.
Residual operator decisions are called out rather than guessed.
handoff_to_auditor: List changed validation rules and authorization decisions.
Identify controls ready for re-audit and controls still blocked on policy.

first_wave_slices.goal: Close storage-side data protection gaps and align submission protections with the ADR.
done_when: Storage-side protections and failure behavior are implemented and evidenced.
Crypto and retention docs reflect actual implementation state.
Unit/integration coverage exists for changed persistence or crypto paths.
handoff_to_auditor: List changed persistence and crypto behaviors.
Identify data-protection controls ready for re-audit.

first_wave_slices.goal: Prepare tests and requirement docs to support Wave 1 implementation slices.
done_when: Wave 1 slices have matching test and docs scaffolding.
Shared requirement docs are updated in one coordinated place rather than ad hoc by every slice.
handoff_to_auditor: List which docs and tests now provide audit evidence.
Identify controls that remain blocked on implementation rather than documentation.

sequencing_rules: ASVS checklist artifacts remain audit-only and are not edited during implementation.
If multiple slices need worker/index.ts, extract helpers first or sequence writes explicitly.
Frontend/browser-facing workstream D starts only after Wave 1 stabilizes the server-side posture.
Re-audit starts only after implementation slices hand back evidence.
